inventionTitles.title: Far-infrared radiation material and medicine and food derived therefrom

address.address1: Japan Kochi
Patent and Trademark Office of China Council For The Promotion Of International Trade

event-title.$: DEEMED WITHDRAWAL OF PATENT APPLICATION AFTER PUBLICATION (PATENT LAW 2001)

section.content: Provided are a far-infrared radiation material and a medicine and a food derived therefrom. The far-infrared radiation material is obtained by steps comprising pulverizing a stone (SGES) having been absorbing solar energy for a geologically long time, emitting far-infrared radiation of the wavelength 4-14 mu m and comprising at least about 28% of Si, about 10% of Al, about 6% of K and about 4% of Fe, and forming the pulverized stone into spheres. This material can be used as sand for a sand bath. Also ultrafine powder obtained by pulverizing the SGES and grinding the pulverized SGES can be used as a medicine or a food.

Far-infrared radiation material and derive from its medicine and foodPresent invention relates in general to a kind of far-infrared radiation material and derive from its medicine and food.Especially, this far-infrared radiation material is a kind of material that the propagation that activates normal cell, suppresses the lipid peroxidation deposits yields and suppress the cancerous cell of leukaemia and transplanting is had remarkable result.

Known stone such as granite, platinum and tourmaline etc. can be launched the far ir ray that wavelength is 4-14 μ m.

The far ir ray of launching from these stones can bunch be dissociated into water molecule.By using ray can remove the impurity of water bunch inside, water is just pure thus like this.For example, be present in the water that these gas sulfur acid gases, hydrochloric acid gas, carbonic acid gas etc. of water bunch inside pollute, can make water pure by using ray, this is because ray makes water bunch disassociation, thereby gas release is gone out.For too by the situation of the water of pollutions such as these heavy metals such as hydrargyrum, cadmium, use ray can cause that inside includes the water bunch disassociation of metal, then cause the precipitation of these metals.By removing the precipitate in the water, water has just purified then.

The far infrared lonizing radiation of the 4-14 μ m of wavelength are known as " growth ray ", and it is to raise the vegeto-animal essential energy.

Recently, the present application people's the above-mentioned lonizing radiation that studies show that activate animal and plant cells, and inhibition lipid peroxidation deposits yields, this lipid peroxide is a factor that causes some diseases, as class pathogenic wind-warm joint class, thrombophlebitis, progressive systemic scleroderma, thromboangiitis obliterans, raynaud disease, obstinate skin ulcer etc.Also proved but this lonizing radiation are used for the human body blood circulation promoting, and be widely used in inhibition paralysis and myocardial infarction and be used for the treatment of atopic dermatitis.And the inventor has confirmed that these lonizing radiation even activity that can anticancer (see lgaku toSeibutsugaku, Vol.123:pp.113-118,1991, Ensho, Vol.11:pp.135-141,1991, Ensho, Vol.12:pp.63-69,1992, Int.J.Biometeorol., Vol.37:pp.133-138,1993).

Yet, from these known stone, granite and tourmaline, the far infrared lonizing radiation of emission do not have a significant effect to following effect: the propagation that activate normal cell, suppresses the lipid peroxidation deposits yields and suppress the cancerous cell of leukaemia and transplanting.

The inventor has used a kind of special stone as described below, and SGES (stone of emission supergrown ray) is to carry out the relevant therefrom experiment of the effect of the far infrared lonizing radiation of emission.The lonizing radiation of SGES have been applied to be transplanted to the cancerous cell on the mice, human blood leukocyte and leukaemia, lipid peroxide.Compare with known stone, find that SGES has more remarkable result, realized the present invention thus.

One of purpose of the present invention is to form ball by pulverizing SGES and with the SGES that pulverizes, thereby provides a kind of with the purposes of far infrared radiation material as the sand of sand bath.

Another object of the present invention is to be ground into ultra-fine dust by pulverizing SGES and with the SGES that pulverizes, thereby provides a kind of with the method for far infrared lonizing radiation as medicine or food.

Far infrared radiation material of the present invention can be by obtaining by following method processing SGES.

SGES as initial feed of the present invention is a kind of long-time stone of solar energy on the geology that absorbed, and it launches the far infrared lonizing radiation that wavelength is 4-14 μ m, and this stone comprises at least about 28% silicon, about 10% aluminum, about 6% potassium and about 4% ferrum.Especially, preferably adopt from the Sobo mountain of Japanese Oita stone.

In order to improve from the amount of the far infrared lonizing radiation of this stone surface emitting the constant surface area of should gaining in weight; That is to say, pulverize SGES.More specifically, with crushing machine the SGES crushing is pulverized with jet mill then.

The SGES that pulverizes has formed ball now.More specifically, with the SGES that pulverizes 1,100-1,150 ℃ following sintering 15-25 hour, thereby form the ball that diameter is 3-5mm (following we claim these balls to be " Ceramic Balls ").

For the sand that utilizes the far ir ray material as sand bath, the quantity of far ir ray more at room temperature will increase tens of times, and the SGES Ceramic Balls becomes warm like this.More specifically, the SGES Ceramic Balls that is heated to 50-70 ℃ is placed in the bathtub, then the hot water of temperature a little more than body temperature or 50-53 ℃ is injected with in the basin of Ceramic Balls.When this thermal Ceramics ball was cooled to the sustainable temperature of human body or 45-46 ℃, the people just was willing to carry out 15-20 minute sand bath.

Far ir ray material useful as drug of the present invention.More specifically, will collide so that wear into the ultra-fine dust that diameter is no more than 1 μ m mutually further through jet mill with the SGES that said method is pulverized.Use contains the medicine of this SGES ultra-fine dust, and for example, for oral administration, the people of the about 60kg of an individual weight can obey the 0.2-0.4g powder every day.

And because the SGES ultra-fine dust is free from side effects, it can be used as the health food that keeps and promote health.For example, when culinary art, can add this SGES ultra-fine dust.Also can use the soft drink that contains this SGES ultra-fine dust.

Find other features and advantages of the present invention easily from following description and accompanying drawing.

Fig. 1 shows that far ir ray is applied to the diagram of the propagation of the tumor cell of transplanting mice.

Below, according to embodiment the effect of far infrared radiation material of the present invention is described.

1 pair of leukocytic experiment of human blood of embodiment

From healthy people's peripheral blood, gather blood leucocyte (neutrophil and lymphocyte),, will act on cell from the far ir ray of SGES or the emission of known stone then so that be placed in the test tube.Check its effect according to relevant five aspects that are considered to activate Normocellular promoting factor: the Ca in (1) neutrophil 2+Concentration ([Ca 2+] i), the transfer ability of (2) neutrophil, the phagocytic activity of (3) neutrophil, the active oxygen (O that (4) are generated by neutrophil 2-) and (5) lymphocyte to the reactivity (blastoformation) of phytohaemagglutinin (PHA).Ca in the neutrophil of [experimental technique] (1) 2+Concentration

Collect peripheric venous blood so that neutrophil is separated with lymphocyte with Ficoll-Hypaque.With 10 7The neutrophil of cell/ml is suspended in and contains 0.1mM CaCl 2KRP solution in, and add 0.1 μ m Fura 2-AM, mixture was slowly shaken 30 minutes at 37 ℃.Washed after twice with KRP solution at mixture, added 15 μ l 10 -6MFMLP.(trade name, Hitachi Ltd.) measure Ca with spectrophotometric fluorometer F-4000 2+Concentration.

(2) transfer ability of neutrophil

The preparation agar plate, the 2.5ml RPMI that will contain 10% inactivation Ox blood serum adds in the 2.5m12.4% agar solution.Direction outside the mind-set therefrom hole that 8mm is the 3mm of three diameters onboard that is separated by: in the hole in the inner part, put into and be suspended with 10 610 μ l RPMI, 1640 solution of cell/ml neutrophil; In the intermediary hole, only put 10 μ lRPMI1640 solution in contrast; In the apertura lateralis, put into 10 μ l 10 -6MFMLP is as the migration stimulant.With agar plate 37 ℃ leave standstill 2 hours after, measure the distance that neutrophil moves from the outside side opening of inboard aperture, this represents the transfer ability of neutrophil.

(3) phagocytic activity of neutrophil

The 0.1ml paraffin oil that is subjected to the human serum conditioning is added to 0.9ml is suspended with 2 * 10 7In the KRP solution of the neutrophil of cell, mixture was left standstill 5 minutes at 37 ℃.In mixture, add ice-cold KRP solution with cessation reaction after, with KRP solution flushing neutrophil surface three times, stick to the paraffin oil on surface with removal.Extract the paraffin oil droplet of being engulfed by neutrophil with chloroform and methanol mixture (1: 2), and (absorb: 525nm) with spectrophotometric determination.(4) active oxygen that produces by neutrophil

With 10 6The neutrophil of cell is suspended in the KRP solution that contains 5mM glucose and 1mg/ml gelatin, and mixture was left standstill 5 minutes at 37 ℃.Behind the zymosan that adds 0.1mM ferricytochrome C and 1mg/ml conditioning, mixture was left standstill 5 minutes at 37 ℃ again.Then, collect the 0.1ml supernatant, and it is joined the 2ml 100mM K that contains the 0.1mM ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid 3PO 4In the solution (pH7.8).(absorb: 550nm) measure the minimizing degree that can make the reductive active oxygen of ferricytochrome C, calculate the amount of active oxygen with two wavelength with spectrophotometer.(5) lymphocyte is to the reactivity (blastoformation) of phytohaemagglutinin

With 3 * 10 6The lymphocyte of cell be suspended in contain 20% inactivation Ox blood serum and through mitomycin handle 2 * 10 5In monocytic RPMI 1640 solution of cell,, mixture was left standstill 3 days at 37 ℃ toward wherein adding 10 μ g/ml phytohaemagglutinin.Finished preceding 24 hours in reaction, with 2Ci/mM[ 3H] add in the mixture.Measure in final 24 hours endolymph cells [ 3H] incorporation.[experiment]

Preparation SGES and as the Ceramic Balls of granite, pottery and tourmaline of contrast stone, by pulverizing these stones and will pulverizing stone formation ball and make.Make Ceramic Balls warm after, carry out the mensuration of above-mentioned five kinds of systems.Check is to the influence of measured value.

Experimental result is as shown in table 1.

*0.01＜P＜0.5 compared with the control, #P＜0.01 ， $P＜0.001 ， $P＜0.0001.α contrast: the value that does not have far infrared lonizing radiation system

Be clear that from table 1 the far infrared lonizing radiation of launching from all Ceramic Balls have activated normal cell.Particularly, far ir ray material of the present invention, the SGES Ceramic Balls, it is the most effective activating in the normal cell in other Ceramic Balls.

The experiment of 2 pairs of lipid peroxidation deposits yields of embodiment

In thiobarbituric acid (TBA) reaction system, the ultraviolet reactive oxygen species of oiliness unsaturated fatty acid, docosahexenoic acid and emission is to produce lipid peroxide.For this system, use from SGES or the far infrared lonizing radiation from known stone, launched.What measured is the minimizing degree of the lipid peroxide of one of factor of being considered to give rise to diseases.[experimental technique]

For measuring the lipid peroxide that produces by the thiobarbituric acid reaction, the docosahexenoic acid that preparation 0.1ml dilution is 200 times.In the thiobarbituric acid reaction, 0.2ml 7% sodium lauryl sulphate, 2ml 0.1N HCl and 0.3ml phosphotungstic acid are mixed, toward wherein adding the reagent that 1ml contains 0.67% thiobarbituric acid and acetic acid (1: 1), measure with the spectrophotometric fluorometer and (excite: 515nm and emission: 553nm).[experiment]

Form ball and prepare SGES's and by pulverizing these stones and will pulverizing stone as the Ceramic Balls of the granite, pottery and the tourmaline that contrast stone, make Ceramic Balls warm after, carry out above-mentioned mensuration system.Check is to the influence of the value of mensuration.

Experimental result is as shown in table 2.

Table 2 Experiment sampleSolventAverage (6 minutes)Contrast 1 (ultraviolet-) contrast 2 (ultraviolet+)Ethanol ethanol ?6.5±0.9 ?462±61 Granite pottery tourmaline SGESEthanol ethanol ethanol ethanol 385±48 *368±41 *245±29# 84±13￥

Docosahexenoic acid (200 times) ten sunlights (UV) of dilution 6 hours.

*0.01＜P＜0.05 compared with the control, #P＜0.01, $＜0.001.

Can know from table 2 and to find out, having ultraviolet docosahexenoic acid significantly to suppress the generation of lipid peroxide (TBA reacting substance) for all experiment samples.Particularly, the far infrared lonizing radiation of SGES Ceramic Balls emission of the present invention, in the far infrared lonizing radiation of other Ceramic Balls emission, the generation that suppresses lipid peroxide is the most effective.

3 couples of leukaemias' of embodiment experiment

Buy three kinds of leukaemias on the market, HL-60, ML-1 and K-562 are suspended in them in the RPMI solution then separately.To this system, use from SGES or from the far infrared lonizing radiation of known stone emission.In order to check the degree of anticancer effect, measure the Ca among the leukaemia 2+Concentration ([Ca 2+] i).[experiment]

Prepare the SGES Ceramic Balls and the Ceramic Balls of granite, pottery and the tourmaline of stone as a comparison by pulverizing these stones and making the stone of pulverizing form ball, make Ceramic Balls warm after, carry out three kinds of above-mentioned mensuration systems (HL-60, ML-1 and K-562).Check is to the influence of the value measured.

Experimental result is as shown in table 3.

Table 3 Experiment sampleThe leukaemia ????HL-60 ????ML-1 ????K-562 ????Ca 2+Concentration (nM) Residue fMLP Residue ??fMLP Residue ??fMLP Granite pottery tourmaline SGES contrast 60.2±6.7 *61.3±3.3 *63.5±8.0 *63.3±9.5 *47.8±5.6 148±16 *145±17 *159±14 *169±18 *128±16 ?33.6±3.6 ?35.2±4.2 ?31.5±4.0 *?28.8±3.1 *?38.2±4.1 ?82.7±11.4 ?84.9±13.3 ?79?3±12.1 *?75.8±10.3 *105.6±14.0 21.2±2.0 *20.9±1.7 *18.0±2.1# 15.2±1.9# 30.2±4.4 58.3±5.3 *62.1±5.7 *47.7±6.2# 40.8±5.7# 87.5±9.8

Can clearly be seen that from table 3, remove the HL-60 extracellular, but from the far infrared lonizing radiation anticancer effect of all Ceramic Balls emissions.Particularly, SGES Ceramic Balls of the present invention makes the cancerous cell inactivation significantly surpass other Ceramic Balls.

In conjunction with contrast, SGES far infrared radiation material of the present invention is powerful to activate the Normocellular while with the result of embodiment 1 and 3, and making equally significantly should repressed cancerous cell functionally inactive.

The experiment of the tumor cell of mice is given in 4 pairs of transplanting of embodiment

To two class tumor cell transplantations that the mice (sarcoma 180 and B-16 melanoma) of cancer obtains be arranged to normal ddy or C from inoculation 57The back of black mice.Use from SGES or from the far infrared lonizing radiation of known stone emission for this system.Mensuration suppresses the degree of the cancer cell multiplication of transplanting with acquisition to the influence of tumor cell proliferation.[experiment]

Prepare several cloth parcel SGES Ceramic Balls and the tourmaline and the ceramic Ceramic Balls of stone as a comparison, these Ceramic Balls are to make by these stones being pulverized the stone formation ball that makes pulverizing again, these Ceramic Balls are used for having inoculated respectively the back of interior tumor 180 and the melanomatous two kinds of mices of B-16.Measure to use the size of the tumor of far infrared lonizing radiation in per five days, so as with comparing not with the far infrared lonizing radiation.

Experimental result as shown in Figure 1.

Be clear that from Fig. 1 the far infrared lonizing radiation of launching from all Ceramic Balls can suppress the propagation of tumor cell, and control the propagation of the cancerous cell of transplanting.Especially, SGES Ceramic Balls of the present invention demonstrates the remarkable result above other Ceramic Balls.

The experiment of 5 pairs of hepatosis rats of embodiment

Give Wistar rat 6mg hydrargyrum (HgCl 2)/kg obtains the hepatosis Wistar rat (female, 24 weeks) of mercurialism.Take SGES ultra-fine dust of the present invention to rat then.Mensuration is to the influence of glutamic oxaloacetic transaminase, GOT in the blood (GOT) and glutamate pyruvate transaminase (GPT), to obtain curing the degree of hepatosis.[experiment]

The SGES that pulverizes SGES and grind obtains the ultra-fine dust of SGES.Take all SGES ultra-fine dusts respectively for above-mentioned three hepatosis mices, every of every day give 0.006g/kg, 0.06g/kg and 0.3g/kg respectively.After one week, collect rat serum in case check to the influence of the value surveyed.

Experimental result is as shown in table 4.

Can clearly be seen that from table 4 SGES ultra-fine dust of the present invention significantly reduces the glutamic oxaloacetic transaminase, GOT in the hepatosis rat serum and the amount of glutamate pyruvate transaminase, thereby cure hepatosis.

6 couples of rheumatisants' of embodiment clinical experiment

Take 0.4g SGES ultra-fine dust of the present invention every day for 85 routine rheumatisants, and the warm SGES Ceramic Balls that further allows rheumatisant's whole body cover 45-46 ℃ is carried out 15-20 minute sand bath.After three months, determine effect obtains the result shown in the table 5 thus.

In table 5, " 3 point ", " 2 point ", " 1 point ", " 0 point " and "? Also checked the minimizing degree of lipid peroxide in above-mentioned 85 routine rheumatisant's blood, the result after three months is as shown in table 6.

Table 6 Do not have and change8 examples0-20% reduces6 examples21-40% reduces24 examples41-60% reduces43 examples61-80% reduces4 examples81-90% reduces0 exampleSum85 examples

Be clear that from table 5 for the rheumatisant, giving SGES ultra-fine dust of the present invention is effectively with the combined treatment that carries out the SGES sand bath, particularly stiff the and arthralgia symptom for morning.Also increased through this combined treatment proteins C reactive.

And from table 6, most of rheumatisants show in the blood lipid peroxide and reduce, and 8 examples only in 85 examples are less than 10%, the rheumatisant make an exception not have and change.Above-mentioned two kinds of results show that the rheumatisant takes SGES ultra-fine dust of the present invention and has significant curative effect with the combined treatment that carries out SGES sand bath of the present invention.

Although described illustration embodiment of the present invention, indicated modification, variation and displaced scope in the description in front, and in some example, will use features more of the present invention, and do not had the corresponding uses of further feature.Therefore, should briefly and use the mode consistent that following claim is described with the scope of the invention.

It below is the resulting effect of the present invention.

See that from the result of embodiment 1 the far infrared lonizing radiation of launching from SGES Ceramic Balls of the present invention can more effectively activate normal cell from other any known Ceramic Balls.

From embodiment 2,3 and 4 result as seen, from those factors such as the generation of lipid peroxide and the propagation of leukaemia and cancerous cell that the far infrared lonizing radiation of SGES Ceramic Balls of the present invention emission suppress to give rise to diseases significantly, its effect is above any other Ceramic Balls.

And, from the result of embodiment 5 and 6, see, SGES ultra-fine dust of the present invention can be cured malfunction, and takes the SGES ultra-fine dust and carry out the combined treatment of SGES sand bath effective to wind-warm syndrome, particularly more effective to those symptom such as morning deadlock, arthralgia, swelling etc. of rheumatism.

Because the combined treatment of taking the SGES ultra-fine dust and carrying out the SGES sand bath also can reduce the lipid peroxide in the blood, therefore it can be used as and cures the medicine that these are considered to the disease that caused by lipid peroxide, as rheumatoid arthritis, thrombophlebitis, progressive systemic scleroderma, thromboangiitis obliterans, raynaud disease, obstinate skin ulcer etc.

The SGES ultra-fine dust is free from side effects, and therefore can be used as the health food of keeping and promoting health.

And</claim-text>The stone of having pulverized is ground into ultra-fine dust.</claim-text></claim-text>10. food according to claim 9, wherein stone is to gather from the Sobo mountain of Japanese Oita.</claim-text></claim-text>11. food according to claim 9, wherein the diameter of ultra-fine dust is no more than 1 μ m.</claim-text>

section.content: 本申请公开了一种检测的方法。当用户需要驾车出行时，电子设备可以获取生理信息参数，摄入酒水参数，血醇浓度参数，采集时间参数。并基于这些参数预测用户的醒酒时间，避免用户酒后驾车。电子设备还可以获取用户的行为数据，身体状况数据和车上行驶数据，并基于这些数据得到用户的推荐驾驶时长，避免用户疲劳驾驶。电子设备还可以在检测到待充电场景后，获取充电站信息和充电汽车信息，并基于这些信息得到第一充电站选项。电子设备可以显示到第一充电站的导航信息，便于用户充电。当用户打车出行时，车机设备可以获取上车前车内图像和下车后车内图像，并在基于这些图像判定出用户的物品遗留在车内时，提示用户有东西遗漏。

The present application discloses a test method. When a user needs to drive to travel, an electronic device may acquire a physiological information parameter, an alcohol intake parameter, a blood alcohol concentration parameter and a collection time parameter, and predicts a sobering time of the user on the basis of the parameters, thereby preventing the user from drink driving. The electronic device may further acquire behavior data, physical condition data and on-board driving data of the user, and obtains a recommended driving duration of the user on the basis of the data, thereby preventing fatigued driving of the user. The electronic device may also acquire charging station information and rechargeable vehicle information after a charging-pending scenario is detected, and obtains a first charging station option on the basis of the information. The electronic device may display navigation information of a first charging station, so that it is convenient for the user to charge. When the user calls a taxi to travel, an in-vehicle unit device may acquire an in-vehicle image before the user gets in the taxi and an in-vehicle image after the user gets out of the taxi, and when it is determined, on the basis of the images, that an article of the user is left behind in the taxi, same prompts the user that there is something left behind.

The present application discloses a test method. When a user needs to drive to travel, an electronic device may acquire a physiological information parameter, an alcohol intake parameter, a blood alcohol concentration parameter and a collection time parameter, and predicts a sobering time of the user on the basis of the parameters, thereby preventing the user from drink driving. The electronic device may further acquire behavior data, physical condition data and on-board driving data of the user, and obtains a recommended driving duration of the user on the basis of the data, thereby preventing fatigued driving of the user. The electronic device may also acquire charging station information and rechargeable vehicle information after a charging-pending scenario is detected, and obtains a first charging station option on the basis of the information. The electronic device may display navigation information of a first charging station, so that it is convenient for the user to charge. When the user calls a taxi to travel, an in-vehicle unit device may acquire an in-vehicle image before the user gets in the taxi and an in-vehicle image after the user gets out of the taxi, and when it is determined, on the basis of the images, that an article of the user is left behind in the taxi, same prompts the user that there is something left behind.

L'invention concerne un procédé de test. Lorsqu'un utilisateur doit conduire pour se déplacer, un dispositif électronique peut acquérir un paramètre d'information physiologique, un paramètre de consommation d'alcool, un paramètre de taux d'alcoolémie et un paramètre de temps de collecte, et prédire un temps de dégrisement de l'utilisateur sur la base des paramètres, empêchant ainsi l'utilisateur de conduire en état d'ébriété. Le dispositif électronique peut en outre acquérir des données relatives au comportement, à l'état physique et à la conduite de l'utilisateur, et obtenir une durée de conduite recommandée pour l'utilisateur sur la base de ces données, ce qui permet d'éviter la fatigue de l'utilisateur. Le dispositif électronique peut également acquérir des informations sur les bornes de recharge et les véhicules rechargeables après la détection d'un scénario de recharge en attente, et obtenir une première option de borne de recharge sur la base de ces informations. Le dispositif électronique peut afficher les informations de navigation d'une première borne de recharge, de sorte que l'utilisateur puisse recharger facilement. Lorsque l'utilisateur appelle un taxi pour se déplacer, un dispositif d'unité embarquée peut acquérir une image embarquée avant que l'utilisateur ne monte dans le taxi et une image embarquée après que l'utilisateur est sorti du taxi, et lorsqu'il est déterminé, sur la base des images, qu'un article de l'utilisateur a été oublié dans le taxi, l'utilisateur est averti que quelque chose a été oublié.

本申请公开了一种检测的方法。当用户需要驾车出行时，电子设备可以获取生理信息参数，摄入酒水参数，血醇浓度参数，采集时间参数。并基于这些参数预测用户的醒酒时间，避免用户酒后驾车。电子设备还可以获取用户的行为数据，身体状况数据和车上行驶数据，并基于这些数据得到用户的推荐驾驶时长，避免用户疲劳驾驶。电子设备还可以在检测到待充电场景后，获取充电站信息和充电汽车信息，并基于这些信息得到第一充电站选项。电子设备可以显示到第一充电站的导航信息，便于用户充电。当用户打车出行时，车机设备可以获取上车前车内图像和下车后车内图像，并在基于这些图像判定出用户的物品遗留在车内时，提示用户有东西遗漏。

A detection method and deviceThis application claims the priority of the Chinese patent application with the application number 202111667026.8 and the application title "a method and device for detection" submitted to the China Patent Office on December 30, 2021, the entire contents of which are incorporated by reference in this application .

technical fieldThe present application relates to the technical field of sensors, in particular to a detection method and device.

Background techniqueCurrently, with the development of society, driving travel is becoming more and more common. Driving and taking a taxi can easily reach the destination, which is more in line with people's daily travel needs. However, in recent years, various driving-related problems have also appeared frequently, such as drunk driving, fatigue driving, trouble charging electric vehicles, and leaving behind property.

At present, for some driving and travel scenarios, electronic devices cannot provide users with effective reminder information accurately or intelligently, and the user experience is not good. Therefore, reminders or services based on driving travel need to be improved urgently.

Contents of the invention

This application provides a detection method and device, which realizes that when the user is faced with some problems related to driving and travel (such as drunk driving, fatigue driving, trouble charging electric vehicles, left property, etc.), it can provide solutions for these problems. Travel tips or services to improve user experience.

In the first aspect, the present application provides a detection method, including: acquiring physiological information parameters, blood alcohol concentration parameters, and collection time parameters for collecting blood alcohol concentration parameters; based on physiological information parameters, blood alcohol concentration parameters, and collection time parameters, determining Predicted sober up time; wherein, the predicted sober up time is used to indicate the time point when the user's blood alcohol concentration is lower than the threshold blood alcohol concentration; and the predicted sober up time is displayed.

In this way, the user can determine how long it takes to sober up after drinking through the detection method provided by the application, which can prevent the user from driving while drunk and causing loss of life and property to himself and others.

In a possible implementation manner, the physiological information parameters include one or more of weight, height, age, gender, sleep time, and sleep quality.

In this way, based on the user's own physiological information, a more accurate prediction of the time to sober up can be obtained.

In a possible implementation manner, based on the physiological information parameter, the blood alcohol concentration parameter and the collection time parameter, determining the predicted sobering time, specifically includes: based on the physiological information parameter, the blood alcohol concentration parameter and the collection time parameter, through the alcohol prediction model, Determine predicted sobriety time.

In a possible implementation manner, before acquiring the physiological information parameter, the blood alcohol concentration parameter, and the acquisition time parameter for acquiring the blood alcohol concentration parameter, the method further includes: receiving a first input; acquiring the physiological information parameter, the blood alcohol concentration parameter, and The acquisition time parameter for acquiring the blood alcohol concentration parameter specifically includes: acquiring a physiological information parameter, a blood alcohol concentration parameter, and an acquisition time parameter in response to the first input.

In a possible implementation, before determining the predicted sobering time based on the physiological information parameter, the blood alcohol concentration parameter and the acquisition time parameter, the method further includes: receiving a second input; based on the physiological information parameter, the blood alcohol concentration parameter and the acquisition time parameter, The time parameter, determining the predicted sobering time, specifically includes: in response to the second input, determining the predicted sobering time based on the physiological information parameter, the blood alcohol concentration parameter and the acquisition time parameter.

In a possible implementation, based on physiological information parameters, blood alcohol concentration parameters, and collection time parameters, the predicted sobering time is determined, specifically including: acquiring alcohol intake parameters; based on physiological information parameters, alcohol intake parameters, and blood alcohol concentration parameters and acquisition time parameters to determine the predicted sobering time.

In this way, based on the amount of alcohol ingested by the user, a more accurate prediction of the time to sober up can be obtained.

In a possible implementation manner, acquiring the drink ingestion parameter specifically includes: acquiring an image of a container of the drink ingestion through a camera; and determining the drink ingestion parameter based on the container image.

In a possible implementation manner, the drink intake parameter includes a drink alcohol alcohol parameter and a drink volume parameter, the alcohol alcohol alcohol parameter is used to indicate the alcohol alcohol intake of the user, and the wine volume parameter is used to indicate the volume of the alcohol drink consumed by the user.

In a possible implementation, based on physiological information parameters, alcohol intake parameters, blood alcohol concentration parameters, and acquisition time parameters, the predicted time to sober up is determined, specifically including: based on alcohol intake parameters, physiological information parameters, through an alcohol prediction model , to obtain the predicted alcohol absorption rate and predicted alcohol metabolism rate; based on physiological information parameters, input alcohol parameters, predict alcohol absorption rate, predict alcohol metabolism rate, and obtain the corresponding relationship between blood alcohol concentration and time; based on blood alcohol concentration parameters, collect time parameters , the corresponding relationship between blood alcohol concentration and time, to determine and predict the time of sobering up.

In the second aspect, the present application provides another detection method, including: acquiring user behavior data; determining the user's fatigue level before driving based on the user's behavior data; determining the user's first fatigue level based on the user's fatigue level before driving - Recommended driving time; display the first recommended driving time.

In this way, users can get the recommended driving time before driving. Based on the recommended driving time, the user can determine the time he can drive. It can be understood that when the recommended driving time is zero, it can be used to indicate that the user is not suitable for driving. In this way, the user can avoid fatigue driving and cause harm to the life and property of oneself or others.

In a possible implementation, acquiring the user's behavior data specifically includes: acquiring the user's travel time; obtaining the user's behavior data at the first moment before the travel time; wherein, the difference between the first moment and the travel time is preset time.

In a possible implementation manner, acquiring the user's travel time specifically includes: acquiring the user's schedule information, where the schedule information includes one or more of the user's ticket information, meeting information, and schedule information; information to obtain the travel time of the user.

In a possible implementation, the method further includes: acquiring the physical state data of the user in the driving state of the vehicle; determining the fatigue degree of the user during driving based on the physical state data of the user; The user's final fatigue level is determined based on the driving fatigue level; the second recommended driving duration is determined based on the user's final fatigue level; and the second recommended driving duration is displayed.

In this way, the user can obtain the recommended driving time during driving to avoid fatigue driving.

In a possible implementation manner, determining the user's driving fatigue level based on the user's physical state data specifically includes: determining the driving fatigue level based on the user's physical state data through a second fatigue model, and the second The fatigue model is trained based on the user's historical physical state data.

In a possible implementation manner, based on the user's physical state data, determining the user's driving fatigue level specifically includes: obtaining the vehicle driving data of the user in the vehicle driving state; based on the user's physical state data and the user's The driving data on the car can determine the fatigue level of the user during driving.

In a possible implementation, the user's driving fatigue degree is determined based on the user's physical state data and the user's vehicle driving data, which specifically includes: determining the second fatigue model based on the user's physical state data; The driving data of the user and the physical state data of the user are used to determine the degree of fatigue during driving through the second fatigue model.

In a possible implementation manner, acquiring the user's behavior data specifically includes: acquiring the user's user data, the user's user data includes one or more of exercise duration, exercise intensity, and sleep duration; based on the user data, determine user behavior data.

In a possible implementation manner, determining the fatigue degree of the user before driving based on the behavior data of the user specifically includes: determining the fatigue degree of the user before driving through the first fatigue model based on the behavior data of the user; wherein, The first fatigue model is trained according to the user's historical behavior data.

In a third aspect, the present application provides another detection method, which is applied to the first communication system, and the first communication system includes a first electronic device and a second electronic device; the method includes: the first electronic device detects a passenger's boarding operation , to obtain the in-car image of the passenger before getting on the car; the first electronic device establishes a communication connection with the second electronic device; the first electronic device detects the operation of getting off the passenger, and obtains the in-car image of the passenger after getting off the car; when the first electronic When the device determines that the passenger's items are left in the car based on the in-vehicle image before the passenger boards and the in-vehicle image after the passenger gets off the vehicle, the device broadcasts the first missing prompt information; wherein, the first missing prompt information is used to remind the passenger The item is left in the car; the first electronic device sends the item missing indication information to the second electronic device through the communication connection; the second electronic device displays the second missing prompt information based on the item missing indication information, and the second missing prompt information is used for prompting Passenger items missing.

In this way, when the passenger's belongings are left in the car, both the driver and the passenger can receive a reminder, so as to prevent the passenger's belongings from being left in the car. Equally, also avoided when the passenger retrieves article, to the occupation of the time of passenger and driver.

In a possible implementation manner, the second electronic device is an electronic device with the strongest signal among all electronic devices detected by the first electronic device.

In a possible implementation manner, after the first electronic device and the second electronic device establish a communication connection, the method further includes: the first electronic device sends the movement information of the first electronic device to the second electronic device through the communication connection ; When the second electronic device determines that the motion state of the first electronic device is the same as the motion state of the second electronic device based on the motion information of the first electronic device, the second electronic device sends a confirmation success signaling to the first electronic device; The first electronic device maintains the communication connection with the second electronic device after receiving the confirmation success signaling.

In this way, it can be ensured that the first electronic device and the second electronic device are electronic devices in the same vehicle, so that passengers can receive the second missing prompt information.

In a possible implementation manner, the second electronic device determines that the motion state of the first electronic device is the same as the motion state of the second electronic device based on the motion information of the first electronic device, which specifically includes: the second electronic device continues N When it is determined that the motion information of the first electronic device is the same as that of the second electronic device, it is determined that the motion state of the first electronic device is the same as the motion state of the second electronic device; wherein, N is a positive integer.

In a possible implementation manner, the second electronic device determines that the motion state of the first electronic device is the same as the motion state of the second electronic device based on the motion information of the first electronic device, which specifically includes: When judging whether the motion information of the first electronic device and the motion information of the second electronic device are the same for the first time, it is determined that the motion information of the first electronic device and the motion information of the second electronic device are at least N times the same, and the second electronic device determines that The motion state of the first electronic device is the same as the motion state of the second electronic device; wherein, N is less than or equal to M, and M and N are positive integers.

In a possible implementation manner, when the difference between the motion information of the first electronic device and the motion information of the second electronic device is smaller than the motion deviation threshold, the motion information of the first electronic device is the same as the motion information of the second electronic device .

In a possible implementation manner, after the first electronic device and the second electronic device establish a communication connection, the method further includes: the second electronic device sends movement information of the second electronic device to the first electronic device through the communication connection; When the first electronic device determines that the motion state of the first electronic device is the same as the motion state of the second electronic device based on the motion information of the second electronic device, the first electronic device sends a confirmation success signaling to the second electronic device; The electronic device receives the confirmation success signaling, and maintains the communication connection with the first electronic device.

In a possible implementation manner, the method further includes: when the second electronic device determines that the motion state of the first electronic device is different from the motion state of the second electronic device based on the motion information of the first electronic device, the second electronic device The device disconnects the communication connection with the first electronic device.

In a possible implementation manner, disconnecting the communication connection between the second electronic device and the first electronic device specifically includes: the second electronic device sends a confirmation failure signaling to the first electronic device; the first electronic device receives a confirmation failure signaling; Signaling, disconnecting the communication connection with the second electronic device.

In a possible implementation manner, after the second electronic device disconnects the communication connection with the first electronic device, the method further includes: the second electronic device broadcasts a communication connection request.

In a possible implementation manner, establishing a communication connection between the first electronic device and the second electronic device specifically includes: the second electronic device broadcasts a communication connection request; the first electronic device receives the communication connection request from the second electronic device; An electronic device sends a communication connection response to the second electronic device; the second electronic device receives the communication connection response from the first electronic device, and establishes a communication connection with the first electronic device.

In a possible implementation manner, establishing a communication connection between the first electronic device and the second electronic device specifically includes: after the first electronic device detects that a passenger sits down in the car, the first electronic device receives the A communication connection request: the first electronic device sends a communication connection response to the second electronic device, and establishes a communication connection with the second electronic device.

Detailed waysThe technical solutions in the embodiments of the present application will be described clearly and in detail below in conjunction with the accompanying drawings. Among them, in the description of the embodiments of this application, unless otherwise specified, "/" means or means, for example, A/B can mean A or B; "and/or" in the text is only a description of associated objects The association relationship of indicates that there may be three kinds of relationships, for example, A and/or B may indicate: A exists alone, A and B exist simultaneously, and B exists independently.

Therefore, the features defined as "first" and "second" may explicitly or implicitly include one or more of these features. The electronic device provided by the embodiment of the present application is introduced below.

The electronic device 100 may be a cell phone, a tablet computer, a desktop computer, a laptop computer, a handheld computer, a notebook computer, an ultra-mobile personal computer (UMPC), a netbook, as well as a cellular phone, a personal digital assistant (personal digital assistant) digital assistant (PDA), augmented reality (augmented reality, AR) device, virtual reality (virtual reality, VR) device, artificial intelligence (artificial intelligence, AI) device, wearable device, vehicle-mounted device, smart home device and/or For smart city equipment, the embodiment of the present application does not specifically limit the specific type of the electronic equipment.

FIG. 1 shows a schematic structural diagram of an electronic device 100 .

The electronic device 100 may include a processor 110, an external memory interface 120, an internal memory 121, a universal serial bus (universal serial bus, USB) interface 130, a charging management module 140, a power management module 141, a battery 142, an antenna 1, and an antenna 2 , mobile communication module 150, wireless communication module 160, audio module 170, speaker 170A, receiver 170B, microphone 170C, earphone jack 170D, sensor module 180, button 190, motor 191, indicator 192, camera 193, display screen 194, and A subscriber identification module (subscriber identification module, SIM) card interface 195 and the like. The sensor module 180 may include a pressure sensor 180A, a gyroscope sensor 180B, an air pressure sensor 180C, a magnetic sensor 180D, an acceleration sensor 180E, a distance sensor 180F, a proximity light sensor 180G, a fingerprint sensor 180H, a temperature sensor 180J, a touch sensor 180K, an ambient light sensor 180L, bone conduction sensor 180M, etc.

It can be understood that, the structure illustrated in the embodiment of the present invention does not constitute a specific limitation on the electronic device 100 . In other embodiments of the present application, the electronic device 100 may include more or fewer components than shown in the figure, or combine certain components, or separate certain components, or arrange different components. The illustrated components can be realized in hardware, software or a combination of software and hardware.

The processor 110 may include one or more processing units, for example: the processor 110 may include an application processor (application processor, AP), a modem processor, a graphics processing unit (graphics processing unit, GPU), an image signal processor (image signal processor, ISP), controller, video codec, digital signal processor (digital signal processor, DSP), baseband processor, and/or neural network processor (neural-network processing unit, NPU), etc. Wherein, different processing units may be independent devices, or may be integrated in one or more processors.

Wherein, the controller may be the nerve center and command center of the electronic device 100 . The controller can generate an operation control signal according to the instruction opcode and timing signal, and complete the control of fetching and executing the instruction.

A memory may also be provided in the processor 110 for storing instructions and data. In some embodiments, the memory in processor 110 is a cache memory. The memory may hold instructions or data that the processor 110 has just used or recycled. If the processor 110 needs to use the instruction or data again, it can be called directly from the memory. Repeated access is avoided, and the waiting time of the processor 110 is reduced, thereby improving the efficiency of the system.

In some embodiments, processor 110 may include one or more interfaces. The interface may include an integrated circuit (inter-integrated circuit, I2C) interface, an integrated circuit built-in audio (inter-integrated circuit sound, I2S) interface, a pulse code modulation (pulse code modulation, PCM) interface, a universal asynchronous transmitter (universal asynchronous receiver/transmitter, UART) interface, mobile industry processor interface (mobile industry processor interface, MIPI), general-purpose input and output (general-purpose input/output, GPIO) interface, subscriber identity module (subscriber identity module, SIM) interface, and /or universal serial bus (universal serial bus, USB) interface, etc.

It can be understood that the interface connection relationship between the modules shown in the embodiment of the present invention is only a schematic illustration, and does not constitute a structural limitation of the electronic device 100 . In other embodiments of the present application, the electronic device 100 may also adopt different interface connection manners in the foregoing embodiments, or a combination of multiple interface connection manners.

The charging management module 140 is configured to receive a charging input from a charger. Wherein, the charger may be a wireless charger or a wired charger. In some wired charging embodiments, the charging management module 140 can receive charging input from the wired charger through the USB interface 130 . In some wireless charging embodiments, the charging management module 140 may receive a wireless charging input through a wireless charging coil of the electronic device 100 . While the charging management module 140 is charging the battery 142 , it can also supply power to the electronic device through the power management module 141 .

The power management module 141 is used for connecting the battery 142 , the charging management module 140 and the processor 110 . The power management module 141 receives the input from the battery 142 and/or the charging management module 140 to provide power for the processor 110 , the internal memory 121 , the display screen 194 , the camera 193 , and the wireless communication module 160 . The power management module 141 can also be used to monitor parameters such as battery capacity, battery cycle times, and battery health status (leakage, impedance). In some other embodiments, the power management module 141 may also be disposed in the processor 110 . In some other embodiments, the power management module 141 and the charging management module 140 may also be set in the same device.

The wireless communication function of the electronic device 100 can be realized by the antenna 1 , the antenna 2 , the mobile communication module 150 , the wireless communication module 160 , a modem processor, a baseband processor, and the like.

Antenna 1 and Antenna 2 are used to transmit and receive electromagnetic wave signals. Each antenna in electronic device 100 may be used to cover single or multiple communication frequency bands. Different antennas can also be multiplexed to improve the utilization of the antennas. For example: Antenna 1 can be multiplexed as a diversity antenna of a wireless local area network. In other embodiments, the antenna may be used in conjunction with a tuning switch.

The mobile communication module 150 can provide wireless communication solutions including 2G/3G/4G/5G applied on the electronic device 100 . The mobile communication module 150 may include at least one filter, switch, power amplifier, low noise amplifier (low noise amplifier, LNA) and the like. The mobile communication module 150 can receive electromagnetic waves through the antenna 1, filter and amplify the received electromagnetic waves, and send them to the modem processor for demodulation. The mobile communication module 150 can also amplify the signals modulated by the modem processor, and convert them into electromagnetic waves and radiate them through the antenna 1 . In some embodiments, at least part of the functional modules of the mobile communication module 150 may be set in the processor 110 . In some embodiments, at least part of the functional modules of the mobile communication module 150 and at least part of the modules of the processor 110 may be set in the same device.

A modem processor may include a modulator and a demodulator. Wherein, the modulator is used for modulating the low-frequency baseband signal to be transmitted into a medium-high frequency signal. The demodulator is used to demodulate the received electromagnetic wave signal into a low frequency baseband signal. Then the demodulator sends the demodulated low-frequency baseband signal to the baseband processor for processing. After the low-frequency baseband signal is processed by the baseband processor, it is passed to the application processor. The application processor outputs sound signals through audio equipment (not limited to speaker 170A, receiver 170B, etc.), or displays images or videos through display screen 194 . In some embodiments, the modem processor may be a stand-alone device.
